author: Jakob He
description: Analyze the current note with an LLM in the sidebar — outline, key-concept explanations, and a distilled summary. Works with Claude (Anthropic) and any OpenAI-compatible API. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Jakob
manifest.description: Analyze the current note with an LLM in the sidebar — outline, key-concept explanations, and a distilled summary. Works with Claude (Anthropic), Gemini (Google), and any OpenAI-compatible API.
manifest.name: Chew It

name: Chew It

releases.author: leweii
releases.description:
- Role / prompt textareas now span the full card width with adjustable height
- DeepSeek default model changed to `deepseek-v4-flash`

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Adds a funding link (Ko-fi) to the plugin manifest, shown as a support button in Obsidian's plugin browser. No functional changes.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Removed the two token settings almost no user could fill in correctly: max output tokens and model context window. Output now always requests a generous fixed cap; input is still auto-chunked across multiple calls for long notes, but the chunk size is derived automatically instead of asked from the user. No new settings to configure — existing saved values are simply ignored.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Fix iOS compatibility: the note-chunking splits used regex lookbehind, which iOS supports only from 16.4. Replaced with a captured-delimiter split with identical behavior, so long-note chunking now works on all supported mobile versions. No functional changes otherwise.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Remove the note length limit. A note longer than the model's context window is now automatically split into parts at paragraph boundaries, analyzed in sequential calls, and the outputs are stitched into one seamless result — each call carries the tail of the output so far plus continuity rules so headings, numbering, and tone stay consistent across parts. Adds a "model context window (tokens)" setting (default 64000) to match your model (e.g. 8000 for Kimi 8k, 200000 for Claude), and the result tab shows per-part progress ("Analyzing… (part 2/5)") while a long note runs.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Add native Google Gemini as a provider, and a quick-preset dropdown in the OpenAI provider settings that fills in the Base URL and model for popular OpenAI-compatible domestic model providers (DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Kimi, SiliconFlow, MiniMax, local Ollama). Also moves the per-perspective "generate with AI" button into the card's title row next to delete, matching its styling.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Add a button next to each perspective's name to auto-generate its role and prompt via the configured LLM, so new perspectives no longer need a hand-written prompt. Default perspectives now ship with an empty prompt (name and role only) to encourage using the new generator.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Fix: regenerating a result tab after editing its prompt in Settings kept using the old prompt text (the tab cached the prompt from its first generation and never re-read the current settings). Regenerate now always uses the current prompt from Settings.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description: Remove disallowed eslint-disable directives flagged by the Obsidian source-code check. No user-facing change.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description:
Maintenance release: address Obsidian source-code lint warnings (typed
streaming events, drop the builtin-modules dependency, use the bundled
`moment`, remove redundant assertions). No user-facing behavior change.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description:
Raise the minimum Obsidian version to 1.7.2 (the awaitable `Workspace.revealLeaf`
used for panel navigation requires it).

**Install manually:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`
below into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/chew-it/`, then enable Chew It.

releases.author: leweii
releases.description:
First release of Chew It.

Analyze the current note with an LLM in the sidebar — outline, key-concept
explanations, and a distilled summary. Works with Claude (Anthropic) and any
OpenAI-compatible API.

**Install manually:** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`
below into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/chew-it/`, then enable Chew It.
